revision.text: #redirect[[en:Senpō]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''撰日法'''（'''選日法'''）とは、[[暦注]]の日取りの方法のことである。撰日法には'''月切り'''・'''節切り'''・'''不断'''の三種類がある。
==月切り== 月切りとは、暦注の日取りを暦月によるもの、すなわち、[[旧暦]]の月の朔日からの[[干支]]や日数で決めるものである。月切りで日取りを行う暦注で代表的なものは[[六曜]]である。
==節切り== 節切りとは、暦注の日取りを[[節月]]によるもの、すなわち、[[二十四節気]]の節気からの干支や日数で決めるものである。多くの暦注は節切りによっている。例えば、節切りによる正月は[[立春]]から[[啓蟄]]の前日までとなる。
俳句の[[季語]]の分類も、節切りによるものである。
==不断== 不断とは、暦月・節月によらず暦注の日取りが常に一定のものである。たとえば[[天恩日]]は常に一定の干支の日となる。

revision.text:
'''撰日法'''（'''選日法'''）とは、[[暦注]]の日取りの方法のことである。撰日法には'''月切り'''・'''節切り'''・'''不断'''の三種類がある。
==月切り== 月切りとは、暦注の日取りを暦月によるもの、すなわち、[[旧暦]]の月の朔日からの[[干支]]や日数で決めるものである。月切りで日取りを行う暦注で代表的なものは先勝・友引・先負・仏滅・大安・赤口を表わす[[六曜]]である。
==節切り== 節切りとは、暦注の日取りを[[節月]]によるもの、すなわち、[[二十四節気]]の節気からの干支や日数で決めるものである。多くの暦注は節切りによっている。例えば、節切りによる正月は[[立春]]から[[啓蟄]]の前日までとなる。
俳句の[[季語]]の分類も、節切りによるものである。
==不断== 不断とは、暦月・節月によらず暦注の日取りが常に一定のものである。たとえば[[天恩日]]は常に一定の干支の日となる。

revision.comment: Created page with "```markdown {{Citation needed|date=March 2011}} '''Senjitsuhō''' (撰日法, also referred to as '''{{ill|Selection method|ja|選日}}''') is the method of determining auspicious or inauspicious days in {{ill|calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}. There are three primary types of Senjitsuhō: '''Tsukigiri''' (月切り), '''Sekigiri''' (節切り), and '''Fudan''' (不断). == Tsukigiri (月切り) == Tsukigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|lunar month|..."
revision.text:
```markdown {{Citation needed|date=March 2011}} '''Senjitsuhō''' (撰日法, also referred to as '''{{ill|Selection method|ja|選日}}''') is the method of determining auspicious or inauspicious days in {{ill|calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}. There are three primary types of Senjitsuhō: '''Tsukigiri''' (月切り), '''Sekigiri''' (節切り), and '''Fudan''' (不断).
== Tsukigiri (月切り) == Tsukigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|lunar month|ja|旧暦}}. Specifically, it uses the days and {{ill|sexagenary cycle|ja|干支}} starting from the first day (Saku) of the lunar month. A representative example of Tsukigiri-based annotations is the {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}}, which includes Senkachi (先勝), Tomobiki (友引), Senmake (先負), Butsumetsu (仏滅), Taian (大安), and Shakkō (赤口).
== Sekigiri (節切り) == Sekigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, specifically using the days and sexagenary cycle starting from each {{ill|solar term's start|ja|節気}}. Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with {{ill|Risshun|ja|立春}} and ends the day before {{ill|Keichitsu|ja|啓蟄}}.
The classification of {{ill|kigo (seasonal words)|ja|季語}} in {{ill|haiku|ja|俳句}} also follows Sekigiri.
== Fudan (不断) == Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.
[[Category:Calendar annotations]] ``` [[ja:撰日法]]

revision.comment: tagging last 50 new pages, experimenting with tagging the ill to avoid redundancy
revision.text:
```markdown {{Citation needed|date=March 2011}} '''Senjitsuhō''' (撰日法, also referred to as '''{{ill|Selection method|ja|選日}}''') is the method of determining auspicious or inauspicious days in {{ill|calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}. There are three primary types of Senjitsuhō: '''Tsukigiri''' (月切り), '''Sekigiri''' (節切り), and '''Fudan''' (不断).
== Tsukigiri (月切り) == Tsukigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|lunar month|ja|旧暦}}. Specifically, it uses the days and {{ill|sexagenary cycle|ja|干支}} starting from the first day (Saku) of the lunar month. A representative example of Tsukigiri-based annotations is the {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}}, which includes Senkachi (先勝), Tomobiki (友引), Senmake (先負), Butsumetsu (仏滅), Taian (大安), and Shakkō (赤口).
== Sekigiri (節切り) == Sekigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, specifically using the days and sexagenary cycle starting from each {{ill|solar term's start|ja|節気}}. Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with {{ill|Risshun|ja|立春}} and ends the day before {{ill|Keichitsu|ja|啓蟄}}.
The classification of {{ill|kigo (seasonal words)|ja|季語}} in {{ill|haiku|ja|俳句}} also follows Sekigiri.
== Fudan (不断) == Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.
[[Category:new pages]][[Category:Calendar annotations]] ``` [[Category:new pages]][[ja:撰日法]]

revision.text:
```markdown {{Citation needed|date=March 2011}} '''Senjitsuhō''' (撰日法, also referred to as '''{{ill|Selection method|ja|選日}}''') is the method of determining auspicious or inauspicious days in {{ill|calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}. There are three primary types of Senjitsuhō: '''Tsukigiri''' (月切り), '''Sekigiri''' (節切り), and '''Fudan''' (不断).
== Tsukigiri (月切り) == Tsukigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|lunar month|ja|旧暦}}. Specifically, it uses the days and {{ill|sexagenary cycle|ja|干支}} starting from the first day (Saku) of the lunar month. A representative example of Tsukigiri-based annotations is the {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}}, which includes Senkachi (先勝), Tomobiki (友引), Senmake (先負), Butsumetsu (仏滅), Taian (大安), and Shakkō (赤口).
== Sekigiri (節切り) == Sekigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, specifically using the days and sexagenary cycle starting from each {{ill|solar term's start|ja|節気}}. Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with {{ill|Risshun|ja|立春}} and ends the day before {{ill|Keichitsu|ja|啓蟄}}.
The classification of {{ill|kigo (seasonal words)|ja|季語}} in {{ill|haiku|ja|俳句}} also follows Sekigiri.
== Fudan (不断) == Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.
[[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Calendar annotations]] ``` [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[ja:撰日法]]

```markdown {{Citation needed|date=March 2011}} '''Senjitsuhō''' (撰日法, also referred to as '''{{ill|Selection method|ja|選日}}''') is the method of determining auspicious or inauspicious days in {{ill|calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}. There are three primary types of Senjitsuhō: '''Tsukigiri''' (月切り), '''Sekigiri''' (節切り), and '''Fudan''' (不断).
== Tsukigiri (月切り) == Tsukigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|lunar month|ja|旧暦}}. Specifically, it uses the days and {{ill|sexagenary cycle|ja|干支}} starting from the first day (Saku) of the lunar month. A representative example of Tsukigiri-based annotations is the {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}}, which includes Senkachi (先勝), Tomobiki (友引), Senmake (先負), Butsumetsu (仏滅), Taian (大安), and Shakkō (赤口).
== Sekigiri (節切り) == Sekigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, specifically using the days and sexagenary cycle starting from each {{ill|solar term's start|ja|節気}}. Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with {{ill|Risshun|ja|立春}} and ends the day before {{ill|Keichitsu|ja|啓蟄}}.
The classification of {{ill|kigo (seasonal words)|ja|季語}} in {{ill|haiku|ja|俳句}} also follows Sekigiri.
== Fudan (不断) == Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.
[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Senpō]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Senpō]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Calendar annotations]] ``` [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Senpō]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Senpō]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Senpō]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Senpō]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:撰日法]]

== Sekigiri (節切り) == Sekigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, specifically using the days and sexagenary cycle starting from each {{ill|solar term's start|ja|節気}}. Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with {{ill|Risshun|ja|立春}} and ends the day before {{ill|Keichitsu|ja|啓蟄}}.
The classification of {{ill|kigo (seasonal words)|ja|季語}} in {{ill|haiku|ja|俳句}} also follows Sekigiri.
== Fudan (不断) == Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "旧暦}}" to "旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}"
revision.text:
```markdown {{Citation needed|date=March 2011}} '''Senjitsuhō''' (撰日法, also referred to as '''{{ill|Selection method|ja|選日}}''') is the method of determining auspicious or inauspicious days in {{ill|calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}. There are three primary types of Senjitsuhō: '''Tsukigiri''' (月切り), '''Sekigiri''' (節切り), and '''Fudan''' (不断).
== Tsukigiri (月切り) == Tsukigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|lunar month|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}. Specifically, it uses the days and {{ill|sexagenary cycle|ja|干支}} starting from the first day (Saku) of the lunar month. A representative example of Tsukigiri-based annotations is the {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}}, which includes Senkachi (先勝), Tomobiki (友引), Senmake (先負), Butsumetsu (仏滅), Taian (大安), and Shakkō (赤口).
== Sekigiri (節切り) == Sekigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, specifically using the days and sexagenary cycle starting from each {{ill|solar term's start|ja|節気}}. Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with {{ill|Risshun|ja|立春}} and ends the day before {{ill|Keichitsu|ja|啓蟄}}.
The classification of {{ill|kigo (seasonal words)|ja|季語}} in {{ill|haiku|ja|俳句}} also follows Sekigiri.
== Fudan (不断) == Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.
[[Category:Senpō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Senpō-qq]] [[Category:Senpō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Senpō-qq]] [[Category:Calendar annotations]] ```
[[Category:Senpō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Senpō-qq]] [[Category:Senpō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Senpō-qq]] [[Category:Senpō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Senpō-qq]] [[Category:Senpō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Senpō-qq]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:q--1]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:撰日法]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Senpō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Senpō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:bumping to try to fix the sexagenary stuff]][[sn:Senpō]][[Category:bumping to try to fix the sexagenary stuff]] [[sn:Senpō]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Senpō]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Senpō]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Senpō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:suQqqqqq]][[sn:Senpō]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Senpō]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Senpō]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Senpō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Senpō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Senpō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qqqq]]

revision.text:
```markdown {{Citation needed|date=March 2011}} '''Senjitsuhō''' (撰日法, also referred to as '''{{ill|Selection method|ja|選日|1=Senjitsu}}''') is the method of determining auspicious or inauspicious days in {{ill|calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}. There are three primary types of Senjitsuhō: '''Tsukigiri''' (月切り), '''Sekigiri''' (節切り), and '''Fudan''' (不断).
== Tsukigiri (月切り) == Tsukigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|lunar month|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}. Specifically, it uses the days and {{ill|sexagenary cycle|ja|干支}} starting from the first day (Saku) of the lunar month. A representative example of Tsukigiri-based annotations is the {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}}, which includes Senkachi (先勝), Tomobiki (友引), Senmake (先負), Butsumetsu (仏滅), Taian (大安), and Shakkō (赤口).
== Sekigiri (節切り) == Sekigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, specifically using the days and sexagenary cycle starting from each {{ill|solar term's start|ja|節気}}. Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with {{ill|Risshun|ja|立春}} and ends the day before {{ill|Keichitsu|ja|啓蟄}}.
The classification of {{ill|kigo (seasonal words)|ja|季語}} in {{ill|haiku|ja|俳句}} also follows Sekigiri.
== Fudan (不断) == Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.
[[Category:Senpō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]]<!--Senpō-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Senpō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]]<!--Senpō-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Calendar annotations]] ```

```markdown {{Citation needed|date=March 2011}} '''Senjitsuhō''' (撰日法, also referred to as '''{{ill|Selection method|ja|選日|1=Senjitsu}}''') is the method of determining auspicious or inauspicious days in {{ill|calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}. There are three primary types of Senjitsuhō: '''Tsukigiri''' (月切り), '''Sekigiri''' (節切り), and '''Fudan''' (不断).
== Tsukigiri (月切り) == Tsukigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|lunar month|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}}. Specifically, it uses the days and {{ill|sexagenary cycle|ja|干支}} starting from the first day (Saku) of the lunar month. A representative example of Tsukigiri-based annotations is the {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}}, which includes Senkachi (先勝), Tomobiki (友引), Senmake (先負), Butsumetsu (仏滅), Taian (大安), and Shakkō (赤口).
== Sekigiri (節切り) == Sekigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, specifically using the days and sexagenary cycle starting from each {{ill|solar term's start|ja|節気}}. Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with {{ill|Risshun|ja|立春}} and ends the day before {{ill|Keichitsu|ja|啓蟄}}.
The classification of {{ill|kigo (seasonal words)|ja|季語}} in {{ill|haiku|ja|俳句}} also follows Sekigiri.
== Fudan (不断) == Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.

revision.text:
```markdown {{Citation needed|date=March 20qq}} '''Senjitsuhō''' (撰日法, also referred to as '''{{ill|Selection method|ja|選日|1=Senjitsu}}''') is the method of determining auspicious or inauspicious days in {{ill|calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}. There are three primary types of Senjitsuhō: '''Tsukigiri''' (月切り), '''Sekigiri''' (節切り), and '''Fudan''' (不断).
== Tsukigiri (月切り) == Tsukigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|lunar month|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}}. Specifically, it uses the days and {{ill|sexagenary cycle|ja|干支}} starting from the first day (Saku) of the lunar month. A representative example of Tsukigiri-based annotations is the {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}}, which includes Senkachi (先勝), Tomobiki (友引), Senmake (先負), Butsumetsu (仏滅), Taian (大安), and Shakkō (赤口).
== Sekigiri (節切り) == Sekigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, specifically using the days and sexagenary cycle starting from each {{ill|solar term's start|ja|節気}}. Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with {{ill|Risshun|ja|立春}} and ends the day before {{ill|Keichitsu|ja|啓蟄}}.
The classification of {{ill|kigo (seasonal words)|ja|季語}} in {{ill|haiku|ja|俳句}} also follows Sekigiri.
== Fudan (不断) == Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.

revision.text:
```markdown {{Citation needed|date=March 20qq}} '''Senjitsuhō''' (撰日法, also referred to as '''{{ill|Selection method|ja|選日|1=Senjitsu}}''') is the method of determining auspicious or inauspicious days in {{ill|calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}. There are three primary types of Senjitsuhō: '''Tsukigiri''' (月切り), '''Sekigiri''' (節切り), and '''Fudan''' (不断).
== Tsukigiri (月切り) == Tsukigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|lunar month|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}}. Specifically, it uses the days and {{ill|sexagenary cycle|ja|干支}} starting from the first day (Saku) of the lunar month. A representative example of Tsukigiri-based annotations is the {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}}, which includes Senkachi (先勝), Tomobiki (友引), Senmake (先負), Butsumetsu (仏滅), Taian (大安), and Shakkō (赤口).
== Sekigiri (節切り) == Sekigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, specifically using the days and sexagenary cycle starting from each {{ill|solar term's start|ja|節気}}. Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with {{ill|Risshun|ja|立春}} and ends the day before {{ill|Keichitsu|ja|啓蟄}}.
The classification of {{ill|kigo (seasonal words)|ja|季語}} in {{ill|haiku|ja|俳句}} also follows Sekigiri.
== Fudan (不断) == Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.

== Tsukigiri (月切り) == Tsukigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|lunar month|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}}. Specifically, it uses the days and {{ill|sexagenary cycle|ja|干支}} starting from the first day (Saku) of the lunar month. A representative example of Tsukigiri-based annotations is the {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}}, which includes Senkachi (先勝), Tomobiki (友引), Senmake (先負), Butsumetsu (仏滅), Taian (大安), and Shakkō (赤口).
== Sekigiri (節切り) == Sekigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, specifically using the days and sexagenary cycle starting from each {{ill|solar term's start|ja|節気}}. Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with {{ill|Risshun|ja|立春}} and ends the day before {{ill|Keichitsu|ja|啓蟄}}.
The classification of {{ill|kigo (seasonal words)|ja|季語}} in {{ill|haiku|ja|俳句}} also follows Sekigiri.
== Fudan (不断) == Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.

```markdown {{Citation needed|date=March 2011}} '''Senjitsuhō''' (撰日法, also referred to as '''{{ill|Selection method|ja|選日|1=Senjitsu}}''') is the method of determining auspicious or inauspicious days in {{ill|calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}. There are three primary types of Senjitsuhō: '''Tsukigiri''' (月切り), '''Sekigiri''' (節切り), and '''Fudan''' (不断).
== Tsukigiri (月切り) == Tsukigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|lunar month|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}}. Specifically, it uses the days and {{ill|sexagenary cycle|ja|干支}} starting from the first day (Saku) of the lunar month. A representative example of Tsukigiri-based annotations is the {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}}, which includes Senkachi (先勝), Tomobiki (友引), Senmake (先負), Butsumetsu (仏滅), Taian (大安), and Shakkō (赤口).
== Sekigiri (節切り) == Sekigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, specifically using the days and sexagenary cycle starting from each {{ill|solar term's start|ja|節気}}. Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with {{ill|Risshun|ja|立春}} and ends the day before {{ill|Keichitsu|ja|啓蟄}}.
The classification of {{ill|kigo (seasonal words)|ja|季語}} in {{ill|haiku|ja|俳句}} also follows Sekigiri.
== Fudan (不断) == Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.

```markdown {{Citation needed|date=March 2011}} '''Senjitsuhō''' (撰日法, also referred to as '''{{ill|Selection method|ja|選日|1=Senjitsu}}''') is the method of determining auspicious or inauspicious days in {{ill|calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}. There are three primary types of Senjitsuhō: '''Tsukigiri''' (月切り), '''Sekigiri''' (節切り), and '''Fudan''' (不断).
== Tsukigiri (月切り) == Tsukigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|lunar month|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}}. Specifically, it uses the days and {{ill|sexagenary cycle|ja|干支}} starting from the first day (Saku) of the lunar month. A representative example of Tsukigiri-based annotations is the {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}}, which includes Senkachi (先勝), Tomobiki (友引), Senmake (先負), Butsumetsu (仏滅), Taian (大安), and Shakkō (赤口).
== Sekigiri (節切り) == Sekigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, specifically using the days and sexagenary cycle starting from each {{ill|solar term's start|ja|節気}}. Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with {{ill|Risshun|ja|立春}} and ends the day before {{ill|Keichitsu|ja|啓蟄}}.
The classification of {{ill|kigo (seasonal words)|ja|季語}} in {{ill|haiku|ja|俳句}} also follows Sekigiri.
== Fudan (不断) == Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.

== Sekigiri (節切り) == Sekigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, specifically using the days and sexagenary cycle starting from each {{ill|solar term's start|ja|節気}}. Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with {{ill|Risshun|ja|立春}} and ends the day before {{ill|Keichitsu|ja|啓蟄}}.
The classification of {{ill|kigo (seasonal words)|ja|季語}} in {{ill|haiku|ja|俳句}} also follows Sekigiri.
== Fudan (不断) == Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.

== Sekigiri (節切り) == Sekigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, specifically using the days and sexagenary cycle starting from each {{ill|solar term's start|ja|節気}}. Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with {{ill|Risshun|ja|立春}} and ends the day before {{ill|Keichitsu|ja|啓蟄}}.
The classification of {{ill|kigo (seasonal words)|ja|季語}} in {{ill|haiku|ja|俳句}} also follows Sekigiri.
== Fudan (不断) == Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.

```markdown {{Citation needed|date=March 2011}} '''Senjitsuhō''' (撰日法, also referred to as '''{{ill|Selection method|ja|選日|1=Senjitsu}}''') is the method of determining auspicious or inauspicious days in {{ill|calendar annotations|ja|暦注}}. There are three primary types of Senjitsuhō: '''Tsukigiri''' (月切り), '''Sekigiri''' (節切り), and '''Fudan''' (不断).
== Tsukigiri (月切り) == Tsukigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|lunar month|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}}. Specifically, it uses the days and {{ill|sexagenary cycle|ja|干支}} starting from the first day (Saku) of the lunar month. A representative example of Tsukigiri-based annotations is the {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}}, which includes Senkachi (先勝), Tomobiki (友引), Senmake (先負), Butsumetsu (仏滅), Taian (大安), and Shakkō (赤口).
== Sekigiri (節切り) == Sekigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気}}, specifically using the days and sexagenary cycle starting from each {{ill|solar term's start|ja|節気}}. Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with {{ill|Risshun|ja|立春}} and ends the day before {{ill|Keichitsu|ja|啓蟄}}.
The classification of {{ill|kigo (seasonal words)|ja|季語}} in {{ill|haiku|ja|俳句}} also follows Sekigiri.
== Fudan (不断) == Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2011年3月}} '''撰日法'''（せんじつほう、'''[[選日|選日法]]'''とも表記される）とは、[[暦注]]の日取りの方法のことである。撰日法には'''月切り'''・'''節切り'''・'''不断'''の三種類がある。
==月切り== 月切りとは、暦注の日取りを暦月によるもの、すなわち、[[旧暦]]の月の朔日からの[[干支]]や日数で決めるものである。月切りで日取りを行う暦注で代表的なものは先勝・友引・先負・仏滅・大安・赤口を表す[[六曜]]である。
==節切り== 節切りとは、暦注の日取りを[[節月]]によるもの、すなわち、[[二十四節気]]の節気からの干支や日数で決めるものである。多くの暦注は節切りによっている。例えば、節切りによる正月は[[立春]]から[[啓蟄]]の前日までとなる。
俳句の[[季語]]の分類も、節切りによるものである。
==不断== 不断とは、暦月・節月によらず暦注の日取りが常に一定のものである。たとえば[[天恩日]]は常に一定の干支の日となる。
{{DEFAULTSORT:せんしつほう}} [[Category:暦注]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[撰日法]] to [[Senpō]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2011年3月}} '''撰日法'''（せんじつほう、'''[[選日|選日法]]'''とも表記される）とは、[[暦注]]の日取りの方法のことである。撰日法には'''月切り'''・'''節切り'''・'''不断'''の三種類がある。
==月切り== 月切りとは、暦注の日取りを暦月によるもの、すなわち、[[旧暦]]の月の朔日からの[[干支]]や日数で決めるものである。月切りで日取りを行う暦注で代表的なものは先勝・友引・先負・仏滅・大安・赤口を表す[[六曜]]である。
==節切り== 節切りとは、暦注の日取りを[[節月]]によるもの、すなわち、[[二十四節気]]の節気からの干支や日数で決めるものである。多くの暦注は節切りによっている。例えば、節切りによる正月は[[立春]]から[[啓蟄]]の前日までとなる。
俳句の[[季語]]の分類も、節切りによるものである。
==不断== 不断とは、暦月・節月によらず暦注の日取りが常に一定のものである。たとえば[[天恩日]]は常に一定の干支の日となる。
{{DEFAULTSORT:せんしつほう}} [[Category:暦注]]

```markdown {{Citation needed|date=March 2011}} '''Senjitsuhō''' (撰日法, also referred to as '''{{ill|Selection method|ja|選日|1=Senjitsu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''') is the method of determining auspicious or inauspicious days in {{ill|calendar annotations|ja|暦注|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. There are three primary types of Senjitsuhō: '''Tsukigiri''' (月切り), '''Sekigiri''' (節切り), and '''Fudan''' (不断).
== Tsukigiri (月切り) == Tsukigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|lunar month|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Specifically, it uses the days and {{ill|sexagenary cycle|ja|干支|qq=draft|qq=draft}} starting from the first day (Saku) of the lunar month. A representative example of Tsukigiri-based annotations is the {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which includes Senkachi (先勝), Tomobiki (友引), Senmake (先負), Butsumetsu (仏滅), Taian (大安), and Shakkō (赤口).
== Sekigiri (節切り) == Sekigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|solar terms|ja|二十四節気|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically using the days and sexagenary cycle starting from each {{ill|solar term's start|ja|節気|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with {{ill|Risshun|ja|立春|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and ends the day before {{ill|Keichitsu|ja|啓蟄|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
The classification of {{ill|kigo (seasonal words)|ja|季語|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|haiku|ja|俳句|qq=draft|qq=draft}} also follows Sekigiri.
== Fudan (不断) == Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.
```
{{DEFAULTSORT:Senpō}}
{{translated page|ja|撰日法|version=93657846|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Senpō]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Calendar annotations]] }}
[[sn:Senpō]] [[ja:撰日法]] [[sn:enpō]] [[en:Senpō]]
<!--Senpō--><!--Senpō--><!--Senpō--><!--Senpō--><!--Senpō--><!--Senpō-->

markdown {{Citation needed|date=March 2011}} '''Senjitsuhō''' (撰日法, also referred to as '''{{ill|Selection method|ja|選日|1=Senjitsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selection method|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjitsu|qq=}}''') is the method of determining auspicious or inauspicious days in {{ill|calendar annotations|ja|暦注|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar annotations|qq=}}. There are three primary types of Senjitsuhō: '''Tsukigiri''' (月切り), '''Sekigiri''' (節切り), and '''Fudan''' (不断).
== Tsukigiri (月切り) == Tsukigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|lunar month|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar month|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Specifically, it uses the days and [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|sexagenary cycle]] starting from the first day (Saku) of the lunar month. A representative example of Tsukigiri-based annotations is the {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}}, which includes Senkachi (先勝), Tomobiki (友引), Senmake (先負), Butsumetsu (仏滅), Taian (大安), and Shakkō (赤口).
== Sekigiri (節切り) == Sekigiri determines calendar annotations based on the [[:en:Solar term|solar terms]], specifically using the days and sexagenary cycle starting from each {{ill|solar term's start|ja|節気|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar term's start|qq=}}. Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with [[:en:Lichun|Risshun]] and ends the day before [[:en:Jingzhe|Keichitsu]].
The classification of [[:en:Kigo|kigo (seasonal words)]] in [[:en:Haiku|haiku]] also follows Sekigiri.
== Fudan (不断) == Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'enbi|qq=}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.
{{translated page|ja|撰日法|version=93657846|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Senpō]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Calendar annotations]] [[Category:qq]]<!--Doing this to bump these pages because my history retrieval attempts have led to category caching errors-->[[sn:Senpō]][[Category:qq]] }}
[[sn:Senpō]] [[ja:撰日法]] [[sn:enpō]] [[en:Senpō]]
<!--Senpō--><!--Senpō--><!--Senpō--><!--Senpō--><!--Senpō--><!--Senpō-->

Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with [[:en:Lichun|Risshun]] and ends the day before [[:en:Jingzhe|Keichitsu]].
The classification of [[:en:Kigo|kigo (seasonal words)]] in [[:en:Haiku|haiku]] also follows Sekigiri.
== Fudan (不断) == Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'enbi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦注下段}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11498765]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} markdown {{Citation needed|date=March 2011}} '''Senjitsuhō''' (撰日法, also referred to as '''{{ill|Selection method|ja|選日|1=Senjitsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selection method|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjitsu|qq=}}''') is the method of determining auspicious or inauspicious days in {{ill|calendar annotations|ja|暦注|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar annotations|qq=|1=rekichū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514953}}. There are three primary types of Senjitsuhō: '''Tsukigiri''' (月切り), '''Sekigiri''' (節切り), and '''Fudan''' (不断).
== Tsukigiri (月切り) == Tsukigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|lunar month|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar month|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Specifically, it uses the days and [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|sexagenary cycle]] starting from the first day (Saku) of the lunar month. A representative example of Tsukigiri-based annotations is the {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}}, which includes Senkachi (先勝), Tomobiki (友引), Senmake (先負), Butsumetsu (仏滅), Taian (大安), and Shakkō (赤口).
== Sekigiri (節切り) == Sekigiri determines calendar annotations based on the [[:en:Solar term|solar terms]], specifically using the days and sexagenary cycle starting from each {{ill|solar term's start|ja|節気|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar term's start|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 二十四節気|1=solar term|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1190993}}. Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with [[:en:Lichun|Risshun]] and ends the day before [[:en:Jingzhe|Keichitsu]].
The classification of [[:en:Kigo|kigo (seasonal words)]] in [[:en:Haiku|haiku]] also follows Sekigiri.
== Fudan (不断) == Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'enbi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦注下段|1=rekichuugedan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514957}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.
{{translated page|ja|撰日法|version=93657846|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11498765]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} markdown {{Citation needed|date=March 2011}} '''Senjitsuhō''' (撰日法, also referred to as '''{{ill|Selection method|ja|選日|1=Senjitsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Selection method|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjitsu|qq=}}''') is the method of determining auspicious or inauspicious days in {{ill|calendar annotations|ja|暦注|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar annotations|qq=|1=rekichū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514953}}. There are three primary types of Senjitsuhō: '''Tsukigiri''' (月切り), '''Sekigiri''' (節切り), and '''Fudan''' (不断).
== Tsukigiri (月切り) == Tsukigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|lunar month|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar month|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Specifically, it uses the days and [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|sexagenary cycle]] starting from the first day (Saku) of the lunar month. A representative example of Tsukigiri-based annotations is the {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}}, which includes Senkachi (先勝), Tomobiki (友引), Senmake (先負), Butsumetsu (仏滅), Taian (大安), and Shakkō (赤口).
== Sekigiri (節切り) == Sekigiri determines calendar annotations based on the [[:en:Solar term|solar terms]], specifically using the days and sexagenary cycle starting from each {{ill|solar term's start|ja|節気|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar term's start|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 二十四節気|1=solar term|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1190993}}. Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with [[:en:Lichun|Risshun]] and ends the day before [[:en:Jingzhe|Keichitsu]].
The classification of [[:en:Kigo|kigo (seasonal words)]] in [[:en:Haiku|haiku]] also follows Sekigiri.
== Fudan (不断) == Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'enbi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦注下段|1=rekichuugedan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514957}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.

{{Citation needed|date=March 2011}}

'''Senjitsuhō''' (撰日法, also referred to as '''{{ill|Senjitsu|ja|選日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjitsu|qq=}}''') is the method of determining auspicious or inauspicious days in {{ill|rekichū|ja|暦注|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar annotations|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514953}}. There are three primary types of Senjitsuhō: '''Tsukigiri''' (月切り), '''Sekigiri''' (節切り), and '''Fudan''' (不断).

== Tsukigiri (月切り) ==
Tsukigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|lunar month|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar month|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Specifically, it uses the days and [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|sexagenary cycle]] starting from the first day (Saku) of the lunar month. A representative example of Tsukigiri-based annotations is the {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}}, which includes Senkachi (先勝), Tomobiki (友引), Senmake (先負), Butsumetsu (仏滅), Taian (大安), and Shakkō (赤口).

== Sekigiri (節切り) ==
Sekigiri determines calendar annotations based on the [[:en:Solar term|solar terms]], specifically using the days and sexagenary cycle starting from each {{ill|solar term's start|ja|節気|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar term's start|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 二十四節気|1=solar term|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1190993}}. Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with [[:en:Lichun|Risshun]] and ends the day before [[:en:Jingzhe|Keichitsu]].

The classification of [[:en:Kigo|kigo (seasonal words)]] in [[:en:Haiku|haiku]] also follows Sekigiri.

== Fudan (不断) ==
Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'enbi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦注下段|1=rekichuugedan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514957}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.

{{translated page|ja|撰日法|version=93657846|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "<!--Doing this to bump these pages because my history retrieval attempts have led to category caching errors-->" to ""
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:撰日法]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11498765]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=March 2011}}
'''Senjitsuhō''' (撰日法, also referred to as '''{{ill|Senjitsu|ja|選日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjitsu|qq=}}''') is the method of determining auspicious or inauspicious days in {{ill|rekichū|ja|暦注|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calendar annotations|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514953}}. There are three primary types of Senjitsuhō: '''Tsukigiri''' (月切り), '''Sekigiri''' (節切り), and '''Fudan''' (不断).
== Tsukigiri (月切り) == Tsukigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|lunar month|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar month|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Specifically, it uses the days and [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|sexagenary cycle]] starting from the first day (Saku) of the lunar month. A representative example of Tsukigiri-based annotations is the {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}}, which includes Senkachi (先勝), Tomobiki (友引), Senmake (先負), Butsumetsu (仏滅), Taian (大安), and Shakkō (赤口).
== Sekigiri (節切り) == Sekigiri determines calendar annotations based on the [[:en:Solar term|solar terms]], specifically using the days and sexagenary cycle starting from each {{ill|solar term's start|ja|節気|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar term's start|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 二十四節気|1=solar term|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1190993}}. Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with [[:en:Lichun|Risshun]] and ends the day before [[:en:Jingzhe|Keichitsu]].
The classification of [[:en:Kigo|kigo (seasonal words)]] in [[:en:Haiku|haiku]] also follows Sekigiri.
== Fudan (不断) == Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'enbi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦注下段|1=rekichuugedan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514957}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.
{{translated page|ja|撰日法|version=93657846|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories|
[[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:rekichū]]
}}
[[ja:撰日法]]

There are three primary types of Senjitsuhō: '''Tsukigiri''' (月切り), '''Sekigiri''' (節切り), and '''Fudan''' (不断).
== Tsukigiri (月切り) == Tsukigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|lunar month|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar month|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. Specifically, it uses the days and [[:en:Sexagenary cycle|sexagenary cycle]] starting from the first day (Saku) of the lunar month. A representative example of Tsukigiri-based annotations is the {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}}, which includes Senkachi (先勝), Tomobiki (友引), Senmake (先負), Butsumetsu (仏滅), Taian (大安), and Shakkō (赤口).
== Sekigiri (節切り) == Sekigiri determines calendar annotations based on the [[:en:Solar term|solar terms]], specifically using the days and sexagenary cycle starting from each {{ill|solar term's start|ja|節気|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar term's start|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 二十四節気|1=solar term|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1190993}}. Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with [[:en:Lichun|Risshun]] and ends the day before [[:en:Jingzhe|Keichitsu]].
The classification of [[:en:Kigo|kigo (seasonal words)]] in [[:en:Haiku|haiku]] also follows Sekigiri.
== Fudan (不断) == Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'enbi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦注下段|1=rekichuugedan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514957}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.

== Sekigiri (節切り) == Sekigiri determines calendar annotations based on the {{ill|Solar term|en|Solar term|WD=Q1190993|lt=solar terms}}, specifically using the days and sexagenary cycle starting from each {{ill|solar term's start|ja|節気|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/solar term's start|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 二十四節気|1=solar term|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1190993}}. Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with {{ill|Lichun|en|Lichun|WD=Q1364685|lt=Risshun}} and ends the day before {{ill|Jingzhe|en|Jingzhe|WD=Q1306596|lt=Keichitsu}}.
The classification of {{ill|Kigo|en|Kigo|WD=Q1085881|lt=kigo (seasonal words)}} in {{ill|Haiku|en|Haiku|WD=Q37707|lt=haiku}} also follows Sekigiri.
== Fudan (不断) == Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'enbi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦注下段|1=rekichuugedan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514957}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.
{{translated page|ja|撰日法|version=93657846|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:撰日法]]

Many calendar annotations follow Sekigiri. For example, under Sekigiri, the first month of the year (正月) begins with {{ill|Lichun|en|Lichun|WD=Q1364685|lt=Risshun|1=Lichun}} and ends the day before {{ill|Jingzhe|en|Jingzhe|WD=Q1306596|lt=Keichitsu|1=Jingzhe}}.
The classification of {{ill|Kigo|en|Kigo|WD=Q1085881|lt=kigo (seasonal words)|1=Kigo}} in {{ill|Haiku|en|Haiku|WD=Q37707|lt=haiku|1=Haiku}} also follows Sekigiri.
== Fudan (不断) == Fudan is a method where calendar annotations remain constant, regardless of whether they are based on the lunar month or solar terms. For example, {{ill|Ten'enbi|ja|天恩日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'enbi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 暦注下段|1=Rekichū Gedan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11514957}} is always fixed to a specific day of the sexagenary cycle.
